channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Trump exempts phones, computers, other electronics from reciprocal tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration is exempting electronics such as phones, computers and other electronics from the "reciprocal" tariffs imposed on other nations, including China.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Hackers find a way around built-in Windows protections
item.description: If not properly managed, Windows Defender Application Control, a security feature with Windows, could be a potential security vulnerability.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba has attended the opening ceremony for Expo 2025 Osaka and expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world is plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: Ayaka Mcgill And Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Reacher season 4: everything we know so far about the hit Prime Video show’s return
item.description: Here's everything we know about Reacher season 4, which beings filming in mid-2025, ahead of the hit Prime Video show's return.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 5 things to know about Meta’s upcoming FTC trial
item.description: Meta is set to face off against the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) on Monday in an antitrust trial that could force the social media giant to divest itself of Instagram and WhatsApp. The closely watched trial carries high stakes for Meta’s $1.3 trillion market value.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Trump’s billionaire pick to lead NASA sparks debate over the future of space exploration
item.description: Jared Isaacman, billionaire, CEO and nominee to become the next NASA administrator, faced questions on April 9, 2025, from members of the Senate Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation during his confirmation hearing for the position.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft study claims AI is still struggling to debug software
item.description: AI is great for generating code, but it’s still underperforming when it comes to simple debugging tasks.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Arm backs big data center ambitions with migration tools
item.description: Arm Holdings expects its architecture will account for half of the data center CPU market share by the end of this year, and to help achieve those goals it has launched a batch of new migration tools for application porting.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: April update has broken Windows Hello for some devices but there’s a quick fix
item.description: A software hiccup has occurred with Windows Hello following this month’s security updates, one that could prevent users from logging in via facial recognition.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: OPM digitizes federal retirement booklets as part of longtime modernization effort
item.description: OPM said the digital versions of four federal retirement booklets are immediately available for download and should reduce delays for federal retirees. The post OPM digitizes federal retirement booklets as part of longtime modernization effort first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network

item.title: Ignoring Wall Street, Apple gets busy on Vision Pro 2.0
item.description: While politicians everywhere seem to be doing their best to make everything pretty difficult, the North Star for Apple remains what it always has been: creating products people seem to love. That’s what got the company through (and out of) tough times before.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Five things to consider before you deploy an LLM
item.description: If the screwdriver were invented by the tech industry today, then it would be widely deployed for a variety of tasks, including hammering nails. Since the debut of ChatGPT, there has been growing fervor and backlash against large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What misleading Meta Llama 4 benchmark scores show enterprise leaders about evaluating AI performance claims
item.description: Benchmarks are critical when evaluating AI — they reveal how well models work, as well as their strengths and weaknesses, based on factors like reliability, accuracy, and versatility.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Broadcom favors stock buyback over investing in innovation
item.description: Broadcom plans to spend its cash pile on buying back as much as $10 billion worth of its common stock, dampening IT department hopes that it might invest in innovation or make its products more affordable.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Microsoft workers say they've been fired after 50th anniversary protest over Israel contract
item.description: Microsoft has fired two employees who interrupted the company's 50th anniversary celebration to protest its work supplying artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military, according to a group representing the workers.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Amanda Nguyen: From Rape Victim to Space Traveler - NewsNation
item.description: Amanda Nguyen, Time magazine's 2022 'Woman of the Year,' will become the first Vietnamese American woman to travel to space later this month. She shares her journey and message of resilience in an exclusive interview with NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation
